macro_segments.title: The Grandmother Case: Allocating Property When Multiple Relatives Are Captive or Dead

micro_segments.title: Setup: Three Daughters, Two Captive, One Dead with Minor Heir
Abaye's First Solution: Give Adult Sister Half, Apotropus for Minor's Half
Rava's Counter: Since Apotropus Needed, Apply to All Property
After Learning One Daughter Died: Minimum Thirds and Sixths Allocation
Final Allocation: Adult Sister Gets Certain Portion, Apotropus for Doubtful Sixth

topical_tags.term: מורידין קרוב לנכסי שבוי (Moridin Karov l'Nichsei Shavuy) - deploying a relative to work captive's property
שבוי (Shavuy) - captive in war, presumed dead
אריס (Aris) - sharecropper, gets portion of produce
נטושין (Netushin) - abandoned due to circumstance (act of God)
קטן (Katan) - minor, cannot work or own property responsibly
חזקה (Chazaka) - presumption of ownership through long possession
קרוב מחמת קרוב (Karov Machmat Karov) - relative of a relative in inheritance line
Grandmother, three daughters, captive and death case
